title: Chicken Casserole
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°.
In large bowl mix crushed Ritz with In a 2-Qt casserole dish layer Crushed Ritz crackers (reserve 1/2 - 1 cup for topping)  pads of butter and shredded Chicken (and water chestnuts if added).
In a large bowl mix soups and sour cream and pour over layered ingredients.
Top with remaining crackers and butter.
Bake for 30 - 40 minutes until top is lightly golden.

title: Dressing
ingredients: 1 - 2 slices of toast (1/2 cup bread crumbs) (if needed)
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°
Mix all ingredients for corn bread in large bowl.
Pour cornbread mixture into 9x12 pan (should be enough for 2)
Cook Cornbread about 20 min or until done and golden brown.
Cook chicken in large pot until done and tender.
Shred chicken into large bowl.
Crumble Corn bread and bread crumbs into the large bowl.
Add onions, eggs, celery, salt, pepper and rest of ingredients.
Add Broth until the right consistency is reached.
Pour mixture in DEEP pan at least 3 to 4 inches deep and bake at 425° until lightly brown on top.

title: Forbidden Biscuits
instructions: Preheat Oven to 450°
Melt butter and cool to lukewarm. (MUST COOL TO LUKEWARM to prevent curdling)
Add lukewarm butter to sour cream and mix well (DON'T BEAT)
Stir into flow and mix until fully incorporated.
Drop teaspoon sized dollop into Tiny muffin tin.
Bake for 10 - 15 min or until golden brown.

title: Beef Stroganoff
ingredients: 2 Lbs Thin cut steak, Ground Beef/ Chuck
instructions: On Medium High heat melt margarine.
Add Onion and garlic and sauté until onions are translucent.
Add Beef and brown.
Add flour, salt, pepper, cream of mushroom and mushrooms.
Simmer on Medium to medium low for 10 mins.
Add sour cream and simmer 5 mins.
notes: Serve over rice or noodles. ( I prefer Egg Noodles)

title: Zesty Italian Dressing Chicken
ingredients: I large bottle of Italian Dressing
instructions: Place Chicken breast in large bowl.
Cover chicken with Italian dressing and let marinate for at least 1 hour.
Cook on grill or in oven until done (about 1 hour)
Brush chicken with dressing while cooking
notes: I remember this was one of Grandma's go to recipes if she didn't know what to cook.

title: Yeast Rolls
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°
Sift flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt and sugar into a large bowl.
Cut in shortening.
Soften yeast in warm water (make sure it completely dissolves)
Add warm buttermilk then add to and mix with dry ingredients.
Turn out onto a floured surface.
Knead just until elastic or smooth
Roll out and cut into biscuit size of your choosing.
Place on a greased baking pan and let rise for 1 hour in a draft free area.
Bake for 10 minutes or until lightly brown
notes: Good with Brunswich Stew (or anything else) always serve with love

title: Barbeque Sauce
instructions: Sauté Onions in margarine until tender.
Stir in remaining ingredients and simmer on medium to medium low for 5 minutes
notes:
Pour over 2 or 3 Lbs of shredded Beef that has been cooked tender.
Pour Beef and Barbeque mixture in Baking Pan.
Cover and cook for 30 minutes.

title: Brunswich Stew
ingredients: Hot Sauce and Brown sugar to taste.
instructions: Cook Hen in Large pot until done and tender.
Cook Beef Roast in another Large pot until done and tender.
Cook Butterbeans, Corn and potatoes in Beef Broth from Beef Roast until done.
Add all remaining ingredients (Use LARGE POT) and cook slowly.
Add more liquid (chicken broth) if needed
notes: Grandma always made this with her barbeque beef and yeast rolls.

title: Beef Stew
instructions: Heat oil and butter in a large pot over medium-high heat. Brown meat in two batches, then set aside.
Add diced onions to the pot and cook for 2 to 3 minutes. Add garlic and cook for another minute.
Pour in beer and beef stock. Add Worcestershire sauce, tomato paste, paprika, salt, pepper, and sugar. Return beef to the pot, cover, and simmer for 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
Add carrots and potatoes. Cover and cook for 30 more minutes. Add hot water as needed if the stew gets too thick.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve with crusty bread and minced parsley if desired.
notes: Recipe can be doubled or tripled if needed.

title: Pin Wheels
ingredients: 2 packages cream cheese, 8 oz each, softened
1 package ranch salad dressing mix, 1 oz
instructions: Beat cream cheese and ranch dressing mix in a small bowl. Stir in green onions and bell pepper.
Spread cream cheese mixture over each tortilla and top each tortilla with two slices of ham.
Roll tortillas tightly and wrap in plastic wrap. Refrigerate until firm, about 30 minutes.
Unwrap and cut into 3/4-inch slices.

title: Taco Rolls
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Brown ground beef, drain, and return it to the pan.
Add taco seasoning and prepare according to the seasoning package directions.
Place 2 spoonfuls of taco meat on each crescent roll, add cheese, and roll up.
Bake for 15 minutes or until the crescent rolls are golden brown.
notes: Serve with salsa, sour cream, or guacamole.
